compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PLAN REFINEMENT (resolving the two blocking plan-review findings).

RESOLUTION A — structural mismatch (G6). Resolve as option (b)+(a) hybrid: the reusable _build-and-test.yml defines THREE jobs mirroring test.yml's current decomposition — 'lint' (make lint + pre-commit + make typecheck + make config-check), 'pip-audit' (the network-retried scan), and 'test' (the os×py matrix: install .[dev] + git identity + all drift/module-size gates + default suite + integration tier). Each job carries the SAME conditional-checkout pair as _optionality.yml (branch-head via actions/checkout when gerrit-refspec=='' ; checkout-gerrit-change-action on the patchset when set). test.yml KEEPS its job-graph parallelism unchanged because the reusable preserves lint/pip-audit as separate jobs — it simply calls the reusable instead of inlining them. gerrit-verify.yaml's monolithic build-and-test job is REPLACED by a single reusable-call job still named 'build-and-test' (needs: [clear-vote, require-ticket]); this splits gerrit's currently-inline lint/mypy/config-check/pre-commit OUT of the 4x matrix so they run once (a net improvement, not just parity). The union of test.yml's lint+pip-audit+test == gerrit's current build-and-test gate set, so nothing is dropped. golden-path stays duplicated for now (out of scope; noted as a follow-up); the 'external' job stays in test.yml only (needs secrets — the reusable takes NONE).

RESOLUTION B — parity test rewrite (G1G2). test_ci_workflow_parity.py is retargeted from 'grep both caller files for identical command strings' to 'assert both callers delegate to ONE shared source, so drift is impossible by construction' (the same invariant it already asserts for _optionality). Concretely, after extraction:
 - NEW _REUSABLE_BAT = './.github/workflows/_build-and-test.yml'. test_verified_gate_runs_every_shared_gate: assert each _SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES value appears in _build-and-test.yml (the reusable), AND assert BOTH test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml contain the string _REUSABLE_BAT (delegation). This proves the gate exists and that both lanes run it via the same file.
 - test_no_drift_script_gate_is_verified_only_in_branch_ci: scan the REUSABLE for scripts/*.py (they now gate both lanes by construction); keep _INTENTIONAL_SCRIPT_ASYMMETRIES empty. The old test.yml-vs-gerrit diff is obsolete once there is a single source.
 - test_branch_sensitive_precommit_hooks_skipped_in_ci and test_ci_redundant_hooks_skipped_in_precommit: point _precommit_all_files_steps at _build-and-test.yml (the reusable now owns the single pre-commit step) and assert the SKIP set there; keep asserting the direct 'make lint'/'make typecheck' steps exist in the reusable.
 - test_optionality_contract_gates_the_verified_path: UNCHANGED (optionality wiring is untouched; vote still lists 'build-and-test, optionality' in needs).
 - test_workflow_names.py: add '_build-and-test.yml' -> 'Build and Test (reusable)' to the locked table so the naming gate covers it.

VALIDATION NOTE: g2p dispatches gerrit-verify.yaml from main's ref, so this change's own Verified run uses the OLD inline gate; the refactored reusable path first executes post-merge via test.yml (branch-head) — watched immediately after landing. actionlint (make lint) validates the reusable 'uses:' wiring + workflow_call input schema locally pre-merge.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Deferred from b550. gerrit-verify.yaml's monolithic build-and-test job HAND-COPIES the gate+suite command set that test.yml runs across its lint + pip-audit + test jobs. tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py polices that copy against drift. Factor the shared gate+suite into ONE reusable workflow_call file (the pattern _optionality.yml already proves) so drift is impossible by construction, and the copy-sync guard can be replaced by a delegation guard.

## What
Add .github/workflows/_build-and-test.yml — a reusable (workflow_call) workflow, checkout parameterized by gerrit-refspec/gerrit-project/gerrit-url EXACTLY like _optionality.yml (branch-head via actions/checkout when gerrit-refspec=='' ; checkout-gerrit-change-action on the patchset when set), taking NO secrets. It defines THREE jobs mirroring test.yml's current decomposition (so test.yml keeps its job-graph parallelism):
- lint: make lint + pre-commit (SKIP no-commit-to-branch,lint,typecheck) + make typecheck + make config-check.
- pip-audit: the network-retried dependency-vuln scan (one ubuntu job).
- test: the os×py matrix (install .[dev] + git identity + Module-size + Prompt-index + Env-var + Docs-index + README-quickstart + CLI-ref + MCP-ref + Security-pin + Criteria-routing + server.json + Public-types gates + default suite + integration tier).
The union of these three == gerrit-verify.yaml's current build-and-test gate set, so nothing is dropped.

Rewire callers:
- test.yml: delete its inline lint/pip-audit/test jobs; call the reusable with NO inputs (branch-head). Keep golden-path, artifact-probe, and the secret-scoped external job (which the reusable, taking no secrets, must not absorb).
- gerrit-verify.yaml: replace the monolithic build-and-test job with a single reusable-call job STILL NAMED build-and-test (needs: [clear-vote, require-ticket]); this also splits gerrit's currently-inline lint/mypy/config-check/pre-commit out of the 4x matrix so they run once (a net improvement). clear-vote, require-ticket, optionality, golden-path, verify-identity, and vote are unchanged; vote still lists 'build-and-test, optionality' in needs, and im-open/workflow-conclusion still folds the reusable's job conclusions into the Verified vote (already proven for optionality).

golden-path remains duplicated between the two files for now (out of scope; smaller duplication, larger blast radius — noted as a follow-up).

## Correct premise: test_ci_workflow_parity.py enforces FIVE functions, not one
It is NOT 'solely a copy-sync policer'. Three of its five functions enforce independent safety invariants that MUST be preserved (relocated to scan the reusable), not dropped:
1. test_verified_gate_runs_every_shared_gate — the copy-sync check. REWRITE: assert each _SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES value appears in _build-and-test.yml (the reusable), AND that BOTH test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml contain the delegation string './.github/workflows/_build-and-test.yml'. (gate exists + both lanes run it via one source.)
2. test_no_drift_script_gate_is_verified_only_in_branch_ci — REWRITE: scan _build-and-test.yml for scripts/*.py (they now gate both lanes by construction); _INTENTIONAL_SCRIPT_ASYMMETRIES stays empty. The old test.yml-minus-gerrit diff is obsolete under a single source.
3. test_optionality_contract_gates_the_verified_path — SURVIVES UNCHANGED (independent invariant: optionality reusable wiring + GERRIT_REFSPEC + vote-needs-optionality). Not touched by this change.
4. test_branch_sensitive_precommit_hooks_skipped_in_ci — SURVIVES; retarget _precommit_all_files_steps at _build-and-test.yml (the reusable now owns the single pre-commit step) and assert no-commit-to-branch is in its SKIP.
5. test_ci_redundant_hooks_skipped_in_precommit — SURVIVES; same retarget: assert lint+typecheck are SKIPped in the reusable's pre-commit step AND that the direct 'make lint'/'make typecheck' steps still exist in the reusable (linter runs exactly once).
Also update tests/unit/test_workflow_names.py: add '_build-and-test.yml' -> 'Build and Test (reusable)' to the locked naming table so the naming gate covers it.

## Validation note
g2p dispatches gerrit-verify.yaml from main's ref, so THIS change's own Verified run uses the OLD inline gate; the refactored reusable path first runs post-merge via test.yml (branch-head), watched immediately after landing. actionlint (make lint) validates the reusable 'uses:' wiring + workflow_call input schema locally pre-merge; the rewritten parity + naming tests validate the structure.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] .github/workflows/_build-and-test.yml exists as a workflow_call reusable with lint + pip-audit + test(matrix) jobs, checkout parameterized by gerrit-refspec, taking no secrets.
- [ ] test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml BOTH delegate to ./.github/workflows/_build-and-test.yml; gerrit-verify's reusable-call job is named build-and-test and vote still needs it; the Verified vote still works end-to-end (confirmed by the first post-merge run + the next change's Verified +1).
- [ ] All five test_ci_workflow_parity.py functions are updated per the enumeration above (three safety invariants preserved, retargeted to the reusable; two copy-sync checks rewritten as delegation+reusable-content checks); no safety guard silently dropped.
- [ ] test_workflow_names.py covers _build-and-test.yml.
- [ ] make lint (actionlint over all workflows) + the workflow tests pass; lands through Gerrit with LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1.

file_impact.reason: NEW reusable workflow_call: lint + pip-audit + test(matrix), checkout parameterized by gerrit-refspec
delegate lint/pip-audit/test to the reusable (branch-head); keep golden-path/artifact-probe/external
replace monolithic build-and-test job with a reusable call (patchset); vote still needs build-and-test
rewrite: assert both callers delegate to the shared reusable (drift impossible by construction) + gates live in the reusable
add _build-and-test.yml to the locked naming table

compiled_state.title: Consolidate test.yml + gerrit-verify build-and-test into a reusable workflow
